description:
**Problem:** When an agent works in a git worktree and modifies `libs/types/src/` (or any `libs/*`), the `node_modules/@protolabs-ai/*` symlinks in the worktree resolve to the **main repo's** `libs/*/dist/` — not the worktree's copy. This causes TypeScript to see stale types and `build:server` to fail. Agents have to manually copy dist files to the main repo location as a workaround (expensive, fragile, takes ~200 extra agent turns to figure out).

**Fix:** In the worktree creation code (`git-workflow-service.ts`, wherever `git worktree add` is called), after creating the worktree:

1. Create local `node_modules/@protolabs-ai` override directory in the worktree that symlinks to the worktree's own lib copies:
```bash
mkdir -p <worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai
for lib in types utils prompts platform model-resolver dependency-resolver git-utils flows llm-providers observability; do
  if [ -d "<worktree>/libs/$lib" ]; then
    ln -sf <worktree>/libs/$lib <worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai/$lib
  fi
done
```

2. Run `npm run build:packages` from the worktree root once on creation so the worktree's `libs/*/dist/` files exist and are current.

**Result:** Agents working in worktrees can modify `libs/` source files, run `npm run build:packages` from the worktree root, and have `build:server` work correctly — no manual dist syncing needed.

**Files to modify:**
- `libs/git-utils/src/` — wherever worktrees are created (find the `git worktree add` call)
- Possibly `apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts` or `apps/server/src/services/agent-service.ts` — wherever the post-worktree-creation setup is done

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] After worktree creation, `<worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai/types` resolves to `<worktree>/libs/types/`
- [ ] Modifying `libs/types/src/` in a worktree + running `npm run build:packages` from worktree root makes the type visible to `build:server` without any manual copying
- [ ] `npm run build:server` from a fresh worktree passes
title: Fix worktree lib resolution: local node_modules symlinks + initial build

descriptionHistory.description:

This causes TypeScript to see stale types and `build:server` to fail. Agents have to manually copy dist files to the main repo location as a workaround (expensive, fragile, takes ~200 extra agent turns to figure out).

**Fix:** In the worktree creation code (`git-workflow-service.ts`, wherever `git worktree add` is called), after creating the worktree:

1. Create local `node_modules/@protolabs-ai` override directory in the worktree that symlinks to the worktree's own lib copies:

mkdir -p <worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai
for lib in types utils prompts platform model-resolver dependency-resolver git-utils flows llm-providers observability; do
  if [ -d "<worktree>/libs/$lib" ]; then
    ln -sf <worktree>/libs/$lib <worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai/$lib

done

2. Run `npm run build:packages` from the worktree root once on creation so the worktree's `libs/*/dist/` files exist and are current.

**Result:** Agents working in worktrees can modify `libs/` source files, run `npm run build:packages` from the worktree root, and have `build:server` work correctly — no manual dist syncing needed.

**Files to modify:**
- `libs/git-utils/src/` — wherever worktrees are created (find the `git worktree add` call)
- Possibly `apps/server/src/services/auto-mode-service.ts` or `apps/server/src/services/agent-service.ts` — wherever the post-worktree-creation setup is done

- [ ] After worktree creation, `<worktree>/node_modules/@protolabs-ai/types` resolves to `<worktree>/libs/types/`
- [ ] Modifying `libs/types/src/` in a worktree + running `npm run build:packages` from worktree root makes the type visible to `build:server` without any manual copying
